claims.text: Outer execution structure and node-level decision strategy are separate, composable architectural layers.

sections: Execution structure and decision strategy are different layers
A more precise map of the planning layer

claims.text: Fixed decision logic is the preferred baseline when the next step and contract are known, while typed fallbacks or escalation handle unmodelled conditions.

evidence_locator.section: Fixed decision logic: keep deterministic work deterministic

claims.text: Production ReAct should be bounded by a step objective, allowed tools, completion criteria, budgets, progress state, duplicate detection, and escalation.

sections: ReAct: choose the next action from the latest observation
Why ReAct loops without making progress
A production ReAct loop needs explicit contracts

claims.text: Plan-and-Execute provides global coverage only when each step has explicit inputs, dependencies, outputs, completion criteria, failure policy, budget, and provenance requirements.

sections: Plan-and-Execute: establish the global structure before acting
A plan must be executable, not ceremonial

claims.text: A generated plan is a proposal that may require deterministic validation, review, simulation, or a formal planner or solver.

evidence_locator.section: Plan validation: a plan is a proposal, not proof

claims.text: Adaptive replanning should run only after material triggers, distinguish local repair from global revision, preserve valid work, and record versioned diffs.

evidence_locator.section: Adaptive planning: change the remaining plan when reality changes

claims.text: Hierarchical planning helps manage large goals but requires parent and child completion contracts, shared facts, provenance, dependency management, integration ownership, and cross-subgoal verification.

evidence_locator.section: Hierarchical planning: decompose goals at several levels

claims.text: HTN planning refines compound tasks through governed domain methods into primitive executable tasks and can be combined with LLM interpretation.

evidence_locator.section: HTN: formal decomposition with domain methods

claims.text: Goals define measurable outcomes and policies authorise or deny actions across every planning strategy; important policies require runtime or infrastructure enforcement.

evidence_locator.section: Goals and policies are cross-cutting, not peer strategies

claims.text: A robust production system combines a Planner, versioned plan store, State Machine, bounded Executor, Verifier, Replanner, and policy layer with explicit responsibilities.

evidence_locator.section: The production hybrid: Planner, bounded Executor, Verifier, and Replanner

claims.text: Strategy selection should begin with the least flexible mechanism that can reliably complete the task and add planning freedom only when its control requirement exists.
